should_trigger: Design an auto-orchestrator execution store where every task claim, tool call, approval, retry, and cancellation can be replayed to reconstruct a run after a crash.
Our workflow dashboard is drifting from the real task history. Define event streams, projections, idempotent handlers, and a rebuild process for the orchestrator.
Two workers race to reserve the same budget for a run. Use event sourcing with per-stream optimistic concurrency and retry behavior to prevent conflicting appends.
We need to replay production agent runs in staging without sending emails, charging cards, opening tickets, or invoking tools again. Design the event-sourced replay and gateway rules.
Create a schema evolution plan for immutable orchestration events after we add new fields to ToolCallCompleted and change how approvals are represented.
should_not_trigger: Make a CRUD admin page for editing user profile settings and application configuration.
Choose colors and layout for a monitoring dashboard that already has a reliable database behind it.
Summarize Martin Fowler's Event Sourcing article for a study note without designing an orchestrator.
edge_cases: The team already publishes events to Kafka for notifications and wants to call that event sourcing. Decide whether they also need an event store with per-run stream reads and expected-version appends.
A short-lived prototype needs audit logs for demos but no replay, projections, schema evolution, or recovery requirements. Decide whether full event sourcing is justified.
A run history contains personal data in immutable events, but customers can request deletion. Design the boundary so replay still works while deletion obligations are handled.

compliance_expectations.should_trigger: Activate Event Sourcing and produce a design or review covering stream model, event contract, command handlers, expected-version appends, projections, replay modes, snapshots, side-effect isolation, schema evolution, and tests.
compliance_expectations.should_not_trigger: Do not activate for simple CRUD, dashboard styling, ordinary pub/sub, or study summaries without authoritative history, replay, or reconstruction needs.
compliance_expectations.edge_cases: Distinguish event sourcing from broker notification or audit logging, justify selective adoption, and handle immutable personal data with deletion-aware boundaries.
